Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement full BLE dive download for OSTC 3/4/5 and Cressi

HW OSTC (Telit + U-Blox variants):
- Full Terminal I/O credit-based transport using coroutine Channel<Byte>
- OSTC command protocol from libdivecomputer hw_ostc3.c:
  0xBB INIT → 0x6D COMPACT (4096-byte logbook) → 0x66+[idx] per dive → 0xFF EXIT
- Compact logbook parser: finds valid slots, extracts profile length + dive number
- Profile parser: versions 0x20/0x21 (original OSTC) and 0x23/0x24 (OSTC 3/4/5)
  datetime at offset 12, depth at 17 (1/100m), duration at 19, temp at 22 (1/10°C)
- Serialised write/ack using Channel<Unit> to prevent GATT op conflicts

Cressi (Beta — v4 firmware):
- Modified Nordic UART: write 6e400002, notify 6e400003, firmware 6e400004
- CMD_LOGBOOK_BLE (0x02) → logbook entries; CMD_DIVE_BLE (0x03+num_hi+num_lo) per dive
- Reads firmware version to determine logbook entry length (92 for fw≥200, 52 otherwise)
- Profile parser: v4 layout — datetime at 12, divetime at 20, maxdepth at 73 (cm), temp at 77

UI: OSTC upgraded to Ready badge; Cressi to Beta; ScubaPro notes Bluetooth Classic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use from (line offset) instead of page for build log pagination (#5)

The Screwdriver.cd logs API accepts ?from=<line_number> (0-based offset),
not ?page=<n>. Sending the page parameter causes a 400 Bad Request with

commit.message:
🔧 CRITICAL FIX: Remove trailingSlash setting breaking API routes

🚨 ROOT CAUSE FOUND: trailingSlash: true was causing all API redirects

The issue was in next.config.js:
- trailingSlash: true forces all routes to have trailing slashes
- This breaks Next.js API routes on Netlify
- API routes were redirecting instead of executing

✅ Changes:
- Removed trailingSlash: true from next.config.js
- Added test API endpoint to verify deployment
- This should fix both email/password and Google OAuth

🎯 Expected Result:
- /api/auth/signup should return JSON instead of redirects
- /api/auth/signin should work correctly
- Google OAuth callback should function properly
- Admin user gdogra@gmail.com should authenticate successfully

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add timeout fallback for ApplicationStarted event wait

Defense-in-depth: if the Wails ApplicationStarted event never fires
(edge case), the 5s timeout prevents the app from hanging forever.
Logs a warning so the issue is visible in logs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: remove accidental tmp file from pages directory

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: disable HttpClient timeout to prevent large file upload failures

The default 100-second HttpClient.Timeout caused uploads of large files
(~100 MB+) to fail with "Error while copying content to a stream".

When the timeout fires, the internal HTTP machinery closes the TCP
connection while ProgressStreamContent.SerializeToStreamAsync is still
looping. Because that method only overrides the 2-arg overload (no
CancellationToken), it keeps writing to the now-closed socket, throwing
IOException, which HttpContent.CopyToAsync wraps as the observed error.

The same upload works fine with curl because curl has no such implicit
timeout. Fix by setting Timeout.InfiniteTimeSpan on the HttpClient;
cancellation is still handled by the CancellationToken passed to every
operation (wired to Ctrl+C via Spectre.Console's AsyncCommand<T>).

Adds a test asserting the infinite timeout is configured.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: skip WorkManager init in secondary processes (R437) (#463)

App.onCreate() runs in every Android process, including the
:error_handler process spawned by GlobalExceptionHandler to display
crash UI. InitializationProvider (which auto-inits WorkManager via
androidx.startup) only runs in the main process, so
WorkManager.getInstance() throws in secondary processes.

Add isMainProcess() and return early after cross-process essentials
(TLS, WebView dir suffix, crash handler). All DI and WorkManager-
dependent initialization is now guarded by this check.

Closes #462

Co-authored-by: ryacub <ryacub@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Frontier-CS: fix scoring, add single-turn mode, and evaluation report (#1141)

* Add Frontier-CS evaluation with fixes for interactive and research scoring

Implements the Frontier-CS benchmark (238 problems across algorithmic and
research tracks). Includes three key fixes discovered through trajectory
analysis of initial evaluation runs:

1. Interactive FIFO deadlock: Fixed bash redirection ordering in
   _score_interactive() that caused both processes to block on read-open.

2. Research scoring pipeline: Replaced broken `python3 evaluator.py
   solution.py` invocation with evaluate.sh-based scoring. Added tar+base64
   file transfer to preserve binary files and shared directories (e.g.
   cant_be_late/common/).

3. Dockerfile: Added python-is-python3, numpy, pandas, scipy, sympy, torch
   (CPU), faiss-cpu and other packages needed by research evaluators.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update evaluation checklist, add create-eval skill, and fix scanner dry run

- Adds separate "Human Ordering" and "Agent Ordering" sections to the
  evaluation checklist with agent-specific guidance for autonomous workflows
- Adds create-eval skill for implementing new evaluations
- Fixes run_all_scanners.py to report sample counts on dry run

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add trajectory analysis results for frontier_cs evaluation

Includes analysis of GPT-5.1 algorithmic (172 samples, 36.6% invalid),
GPT-5.1 research (43 samples, 39.5% invalid), and Claude Sonnet research
(25 samples, 16% invalid) scans, plus custom eval scanners.

Also excludes agent_artefacts/ from markdownlint checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update create-eval skill with trajectory analysis guidance

Fix typo, add evaluation report and trajectory analysis guidance,
remove redundant manual steps section.

* Add shuffle option and increase default message limit for Frontier-CS

Add shuffle parameter to all task functions and dataset loader. Increase
default message limit from 30 to 100 to give agents enough turns for
complex problems.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Frontier-CS: fix scoring, add single-turn mode, update evaluation report

Improve Frontier-CS evaluation with environment fixes identified during
trajectory analysis, add single-turn (non-agentic) evaluation mode, and
update the evaluation report with results from both agentic and
single-turn runs across two models.

Key changes:
- Add PySR dependency to Dockerfile for symbolic_regression problems
- Add interactive problem guidance to system prompts
- Add consecutive timeout early-exit in algorithmic and interactive scoring
- Increase setup script timeout from 120s to 300s for research problems
- Fix regex patterns in checker ratio parsing
- Add shuffle/seed parameters for reproducible dataset sampling
- Add single-turn evaluation results to README (agentic=False mode)
- Fix type narrowing in test_interactive_scoring.py

- Move README notes section below both agentic and single-turn results

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Filter out poc_generation problems with empty statements in HF dataset
- Add Julia runtime to Dockerfile (required by PySR for symbolic_regression)
- Pre-install PySR Julia dependencies during Docker build
- Rename trajectory analysis directory from frontier_cs_1_A2 to frontier_cs_1_A

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(frontier_cs): apply ItsTania's review suggestions

fix: use is_simulated=false row filter for LMS pool instead of simulated_profile

getLMSMetrics and getConceptPoolSizes were filtering on user_id NOT IN
(student_profiles WHERE simulated_profile IS NOT NULL). This excluded all
test users registered while simulation was active, even if their lms_sessions
rows have is_simulated=false (i.e. real CSV-imported data).

Replace the user-level profile check with AND is_simulated=false on the
session rows — the correct discriminator for real vs. synthetic data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(gateway,web): handle old subscription credentials and fix credential panel UI (#284)

* fix(gateway): gracefully handle old subscription credentials missing oauthData

Old subscription credentials (created before credential isolation) crash
the CLI with an unhandled error. Instead of crashing, the CLI now:

1. Tries auto-repair: reads existing CLI auth files and stores the data
2. If no auth files exist: prompts the user to re-login interactively
3. Updates the credential with captured oauthData so future runs work

This provides a seamless upgrade path for existing users without
requiring manual credential deletion and re-creation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(gateway,web): handle old subscription credentials and fix credential panel UI

- Auto-repair old subscription credentials missing oauthData by reading
  existing CLI auth files or prompting interactive re-login
- Extract resolveCredentialEntry() helper for credential selection
- Add repairSubscriptionCredential() to credential-manager
- Fix gateway credential panel showing unnecessary blank space
- Remove redundant chevron arrow from credential toggle button

commit.message:
Fix sidebar layout using correct Hacker theme HTML structure

Previous override was based on a completely wrong assumption — the
Hacker theme is single-column with a full-width top banner <header>,
not a two-column sidebar layout. The correct base structure uses
<div class="container"> in the header and <section id="main_content">
for body content.

New layout keeps the exact same <header> structure as the theme, then
replaces the single <div class="container"> body wrapper with a flex
<div class="page-wrapper"> containing the sidebar nav and #main_content
side by side. Also overrides the theme's ul li { list-style-image } so
the bullet.png doesn't appear in the nav.
